title: Spaghetti Aglio, Olio e Peperoncino
subtitle: The Roman midnight classic, garlic, chilli, and good oil, nothing more
sourceNotes: A staple of Roman and Southern Italian cucina povera. The dish is referenced in Pellegrino Artusi's La Scienza in Cucina (1891) and remains one of the canonical examples of the Italian cucina povera tradition. Technique is consistent across all credible Italian cookery sources.

content.text: This is a recipe where heat management is everything. The garlic goes into cold oil and the heat is kept very low, the goal is golden and tender, never brown and bitter. Use the best extra-virgin olive oil you own; it is the sauce.
Bring a large pot of water to the boil. Salt it generously, the water should taste noticeably salty. Cook {{spaghetti}} of spaghetti until 2 minutes short of the packet time.
Set a large frying pan over the lowest heat. Pour in {{extra-virgin-olive-oil}} of extra-virgin olive oil and add the thinly sliced

. The oil should shimmer and the garlic sizzle gently. Cook for 6 to 8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the slices are pale gold and the oil smells deeply of garlic. If the garlic is colouring faster than this, lift the pan off the heat for 30 seconds. Add the dried chilli flakes for the final 30 seconds.
Scoop out a full ladle of

and set it beside the pan. Lift the spaghetti directly from the pot with tongs and drop it into the frying pan, do not shake off all the water. Increase the heat to medium and toss continuously, adding splashes of the reserved pasta water until the sauce is glossy and each strand is coated. This takes 1 to 2 minutes.
Remove from the heat, scatter over the chopped parsley, toss once more, and serve immediately in warm bowls.

attrs.intro: The two common failure modes are both about heat.
attrs.heading: Troubleshooting

attrs.solution: The heat was too high. Sliced garlic burns in seconds once it passes golden. Start again, there is no rescuing burnt garlic in a dish with so few ingredients. Next time keep the flame as low as it goes and watch constantly.

attrs.problem: Sauce is greasy rather than glossy
attrs.solution: Not enough pasta water was used, or it was added too slowly. The starch in the water is what binds the oil into an emulsion. Add more pasta water and toss vigorously over a medium heat to bring the sauce together.
